scenes.why: explaino_joy base frame (natural attractor reference) + generic.sample Newton basins scanned through the Riemann critical strip; sidecar region covers Im=[-23,23] to show basin geometry for all four conjugate RH zero pairs

sidecars.notes: Newton z^2-z+200.04 -- roots are the conjugate pair 0.5+/-14.1347i (first non-trivial Riemann zeta zero). Basin boundary lies on the real axis; convergence rate encodes distance to each root.
Newton for P1*P2 = (z^2-z+200.04)*(z^2-z+442.17) -- all four roots are the conjugate pairs for the first two zeta zeros (Im=+/-14.1347 and Im=+/-21.022). Four-basin geometry in the critical strip.
sin(z-0.5) evaluated at strip coordinates -- sin has zeros at s=0.5+n*pi which are NOT zeta zeros; control for what a simple analytic zero looks like vs the Newton basins above
